element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Match two or more adjacent puyos of same color to clear

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Single mode - Battle 1 vs first opponent
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Learn opponent attack patterns through sequential 12-opponent gauntlet
Chains send garbage blocks to opponent; mastered through progressive opponent difficulty

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Introduced in Puyo Puyo 2 onwards; SNES version likely includes
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Counter opponent chains by building own chains; prevents short game duration
Final narrative confrontation; culmination of all learned mechanics

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Rhythmic turn-based tension: player action (build chain/clear) → opponent response (send garbage) → player defensive offset. Intensity escalates through 12-opponent gauntlet to Dark Prince boss. Single matches are self-contained cycles with mounting pressure.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial opponents establish baseline puyo matching and basic chain concepts; Arle learns combat patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Opponents 4-9 introduce varied chain strategies and faster garbage block pressure; player must develop offensive chain building
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final 3 opponents before Dark Prince escalate speed and chain complexity; Rulue (one final opponent) represents elevated difficulty due to romantic motivation
notable_spikes: Transition from tutorial-implicit to first real opponent
Mid-gauntlet opponents that introduce faster playstyle
Dark Prince final boss match requiring synthesis of all mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Single mode consists of 12 fixed opponents in predetermined order. Each opponent match is a contained puyo battle. Narrative progression: Arle defeats opponents (most unrelated to plot) → faces Rulue (Dark Prince's ally) → confronts Dark Prince. Establishes escalating challenge and character-driven narrative pacing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Opponent 1 through Opponent 12 + Dark Prince final

notable_level_patterns.description: Endless mode removes opponent structure; player continuously matches puyos with no victory condition. Pure score maximization through continuous chain building. Contrasts with narrative gauntlet of Single mode.

Mission mode provides 52 separate challenges with limited piece counts. Each mission has specific board state and puyo elimination requirements. Teaches pattern recognition and spatial planning under resource constraints.

sources_used: Wikipedia: Puyo Puyo (video game) - Gameplay section, Development notes
